name: Rainbow Brownie Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. For the cookie dough, combine brown sugar, Crisco shortening, water, and vanilla extract in a large bowl. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition until just combined.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa powder, salt, and baking soda. Mix into the cookie dough at low speed just until blended. Stir in chocolate chips.
4. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls (15 mL) of dough 2 inches (5 cm) apart onto ungreased baking sheets.
5. Bake each sheet for 7 to 9 minutes, or until cookies are set but still slightly soft in the center. Do not overbake. Cool on the baking sheets for 2 minutes before transferring cookies to the prepared foil to cool completely.
6. For the frosting, combine Crisco shortening, powdered sugar, milk, and vanilla extract in a medium bowl. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Scrape the bowl, then beat at high speed for 2 minutes or until smooth and creamy. Divide frosting into 4 small bowls. Add different food colorings to each bowl, adding one drop at a time, until the desired shade is reached (a soft pastel color can be achieved with just one or two drops). Frost cookies and sprinkle with coordinating colored sugar crystals. Makes: About 3 dozen cookies
